description: Edges, passages, and transitional zones between regions.
regions: Isthmus
A precarious ribbon of land bridging two continents, lashed by waves on either side.
Corridor
A narrow strip of territory threading between hostile lands, vital for trade and troop movement.
Passage
A hard-won route through otherwise impassable terrain, known to guides and smugglers alike.
Gateway
A natural or fortified entry point commanding access to the lands beyond.
Threshold
The liminal boundary where one world ends and another begins, felt before it is seen.
Frontier
The ragged edge of settled land, where farmsteads thin out and wilderness takes hold.
Periphery
The outermost ring of a realm's influence, where loyalty fades and rival powers encroach.
Fringe
The wild, unsettled margin where civilization frays into untamed country.
Rim
A raised or defining edge encircling a region, often a ridge or cliff that marks the border.
Edge
The sharp boundary where one kind of land gives way abruptly to another.
Verge
The narrow transitional strip just before a dramatic change in terrain or rule.
Margin
A thin, contested border zone belonging fully to neither side, shifting with every season.
